case_title: Bachan Singh v. State of Punjab
subject: Constitutionality of death penalty
outcome: Death penalty upheld but restricted to rarest of rare cases
key_holdings: Death penalty does not violate Article 21 of the Constitution
Life imprisonment is the rule; death penalty is the exception
Courts must balance aggravating and mitigating circumstances
The rarest of rare test established

subject: Sexual harassment at workplace
key_holdings: Sexual harassment at workplace violates Article 21
Guidelines for prevention of sexual harassment laid down
Employers must provide safe working environment
Foundation for Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace Act, 2013

case_title: Shreya Singhal v. Union of India
subject: Constitutionality of Section 66A IT Act
key_holdings: Section 66A violates Article 19(1)(a) — freedom of speech
Distinction between discussion, advocacy, and incitement
Online speech has same protection as offline speech
Section 69A and 79 read down

case_title: Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala
subject: Basic structure doctrine
key_holdings: Parliament cannot alter the basic structure of the Constitution
Basic structure includes: democracy, federalism, secularism, judicial review
Amendment power under Article 368 is not unlimited
Judicial review is part of the basic structure

case_title: Maneka Gandhi v. Union of India
subject: Due process and Article 21
key_holdings: Article 21 is not merely about executive action but also about procedure established by law
Procedure must be just, fair, and reasonable
Article 14, 19, and 21 are interrelated
Right to travel abroad is part of personal liberty
